text.title: API Versioning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-versioning artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: An API MUST use exactly one versioning scheme (url-path OR accept-header OR header-key). Mixing schemes is forbidden.

rationale.source: Stripe / GitHub versioning guidance + Internal — versioning audit

rule.rationale: Mixed schemes force consumers to negotiate version twice; tooling cannot infer the canonical version.

rule.statement: Any breaking change MUST trigger a major version bump (v1→v2). Additive changes use minor; docs-only use patch.
rule.rationale: Without a bump, clients break silently; with the bump, clients opt-in to the new version.

rule.statement: Every deprecated version MUST declare a Sunset header (RFC 8594) with an ISO date AND a deprecation-window of >= 90 days for public APIs.

rationale.source: RFC 8594 Sunset Header + RFC 8288 Web Linking

rule.rationale: Partners need time to migrate; ad-hoc "we'll sunset soon" announcements are not actionable.

rule.statement: A change is breaking if it removes/renames a field, tightens validation, removes/renames a status code, changes a response shape, or makes an optional field required. Adding optional fields or new endpoints is additive.
rule.rationale: Without a clear classifier, every team invents their own; tooling cannot enforce.

rule.statement: Version MUST be carried in URL path / Accept header / header key — NOT in the request body. Body-level versioning is forbidden.

rationale.source: Internal — versioning audit + REST design conventions

rule.rationale: Body-level versioning prevents proxies and caches from routing by version; also lets one client mix versions per call.
